# Marimo Audio Self-Tutorial

This book is a guided companion to the Marimo notebooks in this repository. The
notebooks are deliberately small, but the chapters give the teaching frame:
what each notebook is trying to show, what students should notice, and how an
instructor might respond to the work.

The course moves from one generated sound to a simplified version of Annabelle's
music-to-sheet-music workflow:

- generate and hear audio
- add controls and reactive cells
- visualize waveforms and spectra
- detect pitches and note starts
- represent transcription as inspectable tables
- render notation
- compare output against ground truth
- think about deployment as a public app

## Two Ways to Use the Materials

**Run locally.** Students who know how to use a terminal can clone the repo and
open any notebook with Marimo:

```bash
uv sync
uv run marimo edit notebooks/01_sine_wave/notebook.py
```

**Use browser notebooks where available.** Lightweight notebooks are also
exported as editable Marimo WASM pages. Those run in the browser from GitHub Pages. Later
notebooks use heavier audio and notation packages, so the book treats those as
local-first or Hugging Face Space candidates.

## Instructor Use

Use each chapter as a feedback guide. The point is not just whether the code
runs; the point is whether the student can explain the pipeline:

- what data enters this step
- what transformation happens
- what output is inspectable
- what limitation or failure mode appears
- what the next design move should be
